content.full_text:
August 31, 1992

Scott Hallabrin, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

I am writing on behalf of a planning commissioner of the City of Gardena who requests advice on whether he can participate in the decision-making process of the commission with respect to a particular project.

The City intends to financially assist a local YMCA in relocating to a larger site in the City which is presently owned by the City and will be sold to the YMCA. Certain matters pertaining to the development of the project, most likely including a site plan review, will be decided upon by the planning commission.

While the commissioner owns and occupies a single family home within a 300 foot radius on the west side of this east/west elongated site, he is in excess of 500 feet from any proposed building. Neither does he abut the proposed site. He feels he is fully capable of acting in an impartial manner, concerned only with the best interests of the City as a whole, and further does not feel that the project will have any financial impact on him regardless of the ultimate decision.

He would appreciate advice from the Commission on whether the effect of a decision by the planning commission would have a material effect on his real property so as to disqualify him from taking part in that decision.

Thank you in advance for your cooperation.

Very truly yours,

Michael J. Karger
City Attorney
